To ensure that parts reach the production line as per plan. Proactively inform assembly about the model mix as perpart availability.

Internal

2. Knowledge of Material accounting / PIV (Perpetual Inventory Verifi cation) systemand take care of external / internal audits
3. Ensure availability of capacity at vendor end to fulfi l the required components asper plan
4. Knowledge about various manufacturing processes and systems required forautomobile products, mainly Press components and fabrication components
5.Knowledge of inventory management and stores management function and systemsrelated to SAP- MM & PP module

5. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
1.Plan and procure vendor components as per monthly production plan of models of Motorcycles.
2. Ensure availability of components as per shift wise / daily production plan in terms of Quantity and Quality
3. Plan and execute changes in production plan related with cross functional departments in the plant and Vendors
4.Plan and coordinate smooth receipt and issue of vendor components to user dept in the plant as perrequirement, to avoid losses.
5. Monitor Inventory and control within management norms.
6.Plan and execute outsourcing activity as per management guidelines.
7.Monitor transport cost of vendor material and target to reduce by applying various techniques e.g. Milk routerun, 3PL (Third party logistics) etc.
8.Plan and resolve GST related issues in material procurement.

Job Description
Job Title:
AM/MGR (VEHICLE ASSEMBLY CVD)
Level:
EX- L3
Function:
Manufacturing Operations
Department (Sub-function):
ASSEMBLY – CVD (VEHICLE ASSEMBLY)
Office/Location:
Waluj – Aurangabad – Commercial Vehicle Plant
Ensure smooth operations of Commercial Vehicle Assembly Shop and meet the daily / weekly / monthly / yearly production target.
Daily ~1.5K – 2.5K vehicles are produced and supplied to domestic and export market. Rigorous monitoring of PQCDSME parameters for continuous improvement through TPM Way of working. Continuous interface with cross functional departments like Engine Assembly, paint shop, ME, QA, PPC & vendors for related challenges and get their support to achieve PQCDSME targets.
Closely interaction with cell members working on various conveyor lines in vehicle assembly to achieve daily output and resolve labour issues and support them for removing their pain areas.  Own/support activities related to New Model Introduction, Output/man/DPR/RFT/Flexibility Improvement, VAVE Proposals, New Technology Adaption, Engineering change (ECN) Implementation etc. On job training and development of newcomers.
Cell member strength of FA CVD is @ 700, divided in two shifts.    FA has 2 conveyors. Production is done of 2 shift basis and managed by 2-3 Managers.  Position requires working in Shifts.

Direct Reports
LX
@ 200-300 IN SHIFT.

Position Title: Corporate VIP / Directors’ Driver
Work Location: Corporate Office / Assigned Travel Routes
Department: Administration / Support Services
Reports To: Admin Manager / Transport Supervisor
To provide safe, reliable, and confidential transportation services for company Directors, Executive Leadership, and VIP guests while maintaining professionalism, vehicle safety, and strong defensive driving practices.
• Drive Directors, VIP guests, and senior executives to meetings, events, airports, and scheduled destinations.
• Ensure punctuality, effective route planning, and timely arrivals considering traffic and weather conditions.
• Demonstrate professional etiquette and provide superior customer service.
• Apply defensive driving techniques, anticipate hazards, and maintain safe distances.
• Follow all traffic laws, company driving policies, and adhere to speed regulations.
• Use navigation tools to identify safe routes and stay updated on traffic or road diversions.
• Conduct daily inspections (fuel, oil, brakes, lights, tires, AC).
• Ensure the vehicle is clean and presentable at all times.
• Coordinate servicing, maintenance, and repairs with the admin team.
• Maintain vehicle logs for fuel, mileage, and service intervals.
• Follow corporate VIP handling protocols and etiquette.
• Maintain confidentiality regarding discussions, routes, and sensitive information.
• Assist with luggage handling and ensure passenger comfort and safety.
• Maintain a valid LMV driving license with a clean record.
• Ensure availability of required vehicle documents such as RC, insurance, PUC, and emergency kits.
• Comply with company security procedures, gate passes, and transport policies.
• Minimum 10th / 12th pass (as per company norms).
• Valid LMV driving license with a clean driving record.
• Certification in Defensive Driving (preferred).
• Familiarity with road safety standards and luxury vehicle handling.
5–10 years of experience as a corporate driver or chauffeur for senior management and VIP personnel.
• Excellent knowledge of city routes, highways, airports, and commercial zones.
• Strong defensive driving and risk awareness abilities.
• Professional communication, grooming, and disciplined behavior.
• Ability to work flexible hours, including late evenings and weekends.
• High integrity, confidentiality, and reliability.

Technical/Functional
1. Dealership management
2. Workshop management
3. Total Productivity Management
4. Product technical knowledge
5. Market knowledge
6. Legal knowledge
7. Basics of finance
8. Project management
Behavioural
L3 - ACT - Continuously raise the bar; Ensure results with speed; Meet customer expectations
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
a)            Dealership profitability
1.            Planning and monitoring the achievement of the service targets in the area
2.            Execution of primary service network (dealer workshops & branches) and secondary service network (Certified Service Technicians and Power Technicians) expansion activities
3.            Identification of service gaps and recommend new dealer workshops/branches
4.            Promoting the sales of Bajaj Genuine Spares and Bajaj Genuine Oil
5.            Promoting referral sales by providing service support
6.            Handholding of dealers for the achievement of customer retention targets and overall dealership profitability
b)            Capability building
1.            Guiding dealers in the region with respect to monitoring & handling dealer network
2.            Identifies training requirements and organizes training for dealership manpower
3.            Gather and disseminate market intelligence by keeping track of competitor products as well as services
4.            Coordinate with dealers in manpower recruitment for workshops
5.            Execution of projects in the area as determined by the RSM/H.O. from time-to-time
6.            Initiate marketing programs/ BTL activities from time to time in the assigned region and monitor customer service to achieve service objectives and customer satisfaction
7.            Kaizens and Stock planning & correction
c)            Systems & processes
1.            Ensures the adherence to the company’s service standards by all dealer workshops & branches across the region
2.            Implementation of Total Productivity Management way of working
3.            Implementation and audit of Service Quality Systems
4.            Conduct Dealer Service Standard (DSS) audit and ensure 100% compliance of all dealers; initiate corrective actions for non-conforming dealerships
5.            Work on reduction in service turn-around time and no. of repeat jobs, minimize cost/time deviations, etc.
d)            Customer relationship management
1.            Enhancing the customer satisfaction levels in all dealer workshops
2.            Guiding dealers in speedy resolution of critical customer issues and work for prevention of such issues
3.            Coordinate with the service support team for the speedy resolution of customer complaints on the company website and consumer forums/legal cases
e)            Product support
1.            Continuously get feedback about product performance & product failures, perform root cause analysis, and update the manufacturing and R&D teams through RSM
2.            Involve in planning and executing new product launches and conduct pre-launch field trials
The above list is not exhaustive and could evolve with changing needs & priorities of the company

Functional reporting to  :  SECURITY MANAGER
3
Background
i) Educational Qualification
Should have worked as Fire Officer in Government / Private Sector for at least 05 years
Desirable:
- Diploma in Fire Safety from State Board of Technical Education / Diploma in Fire Engineering.
- BSc / B(Tech) with at least 5 years Industry Experience.
- First Aid & Safety Course from State Fire Trg Centre.
- Basic computer skills in MS Excel, Word, Office for initiating reports.
- Can READ, WRITE & SPEAK Marathi, Hindi & English.
- HMV Driving License & experience in driving Fire Tender.
ii) Experience
Minimum :  5 years as Fire Officer
Maximum : 15 years as Fire Officer
iii) Age Limit
Minimum :  38 years
Maximum :  45 years
4
Job Role & Responsibilities
Role :
- Ensure Fire Safety at BAL Akurdi Premises & provide advisory support to other Plants.
- To lead Fire Dept & coordinate duties of Fm, allocate responsibilities to cover entire premises, monitor upkeep of fire appliances.
- Liaise with PCMC & other Govt agencies for obtaining Fire NOC, license renewals, calibration of pressure gauges, Weigh Bridge, etc.
- Liaise with Police / PCMC / other Govt agencies to process Fire-calls, Range Allocation, Wireless Telephony License Renewals, Weapon License Renewals, Lockdown Permits, lodging FIRs / Complaints. - Coordinate Mutual Fire Fighting Scheme with PCMC & neighboring Companies.
- Conduct Refresher Fire Trg for all on-roll Fm of BAL Akurdi & Chakan Plants.
- Advice Mgt regarding fire appliances to be installed at new complexes & revise existing installations.
- Operate as SHE Captain for Security & Fire Division and update Dept Manual, HIRA, Legal Compliance aspects. Prepare Division for OHSAS / IMS Certification / Recertification / Surveillance Audits.
- Provide Fire & Security support for Helicopter operations and safety during parking at Hangar.
- Operate as Shift-in-charge 1st, 2nd, 3rd Shifts & Supervise Guards (attendance, relief, discipline, alertness).
- Monitor Access (Man & Material) & patrol boundary / sensitive areas in premises.
- Apart from Shift-time, be on-call to attend any emergency (medical, fire, IR, accident, etc).
Responsibilities :
- Retain fire equipment in operational readiness state at all times, viz :- 02 Fire Tenders, @ 500 Fire extinguishers, Companywide installed fire hydrants, fire detection, alarm, suppression & firefighting systems at various complexes of Corporate, R&D, PE, LPG, Pump house, GTE Hostel, Helipad, Offices of various Depts, BFL(AF), Koregaon Park, etc.
- Coordinate AMC of fire-extinguishers & all fire-appliances in BAL premises & Koregaon Park.
- Plan & coordinate relief of Fm when required for site visits for AMC, Petrol / Diesel decantation, Installation / Commissioning of fire appliances in new projects / offices, etc.
- Conduct Refresher Fire Fighting Trg for all staff of Security Dept as also train employees & contract labor of all Depts, Offices, R&D test cells, GTE Hostel, BFL(AF), PE, Chakan Plants, etc.
- Coordinate Fire Evacuation Emergency Drills across the Plant & important complexes, viz :- Corporate, R&D, PE, BFL(AF), HR etc.
- Attend Fire Calls within Plant & also provide support to PCMC Fire Brigade in case of an emergency.
- To attend to other allied functions viz :- assist if employees are caught in closed lifts, clearance of bee-hives, pest control of hangar, snake / other animals disposal, etc.
- Issue Car Stickers, monitor Road Safety & Parking norms in premises.
- Assist CSO in conduct of Security Staff’s Annual Trg & ensure fitness.
- Monitor Security & Fire Staff activities / interactions to prevent any non-conformance.
- Administration of Security & Fire Division to include :- procurement & issue of uniforms, seasonal wear, Team kit, Dept assets & conduct preventive maintenance.
- Maintain records & ensure upkeep of Dept vehicles with emphasis on Legal Compliances.
- FO is custodian of all confidential documents, files, material impounded during enquiries.
5
Competency Requirements
Technical / Functional :
-  Sound knowledge of Access Control mechanism / process, Remote Surveillance, Control Room Operations & Material Movement.
-  Understand contemporary Fire-Safety operations, use of gadgets & Emergency processes.
Behavioral :
-  High degree of awareness & alertness on / off duty.
- Supportive in nature, inquisitive & have eye for detail.
- To reside inside BAJAJ Vihar & family to be able to adjust to others.
- Have a very high sense of commitment, dedication to duty & have positive mind-set.
- Preferably be Marathi speaking from Maharashtra State.
- To be physically & be medically FIT to take-on strenuous activity.
- Clear discipline record from previous organization.
- NO negative Service / Police Record, Court Cases, other distractions.
- Non-alcoholic.
- Have maturity to lead current Team of Security Staff.
Specific expertise required for functional area :
- Security Operations, viz :- Industrial Security experience Operations of fire-extinguishers, fire hydrants / hose, fire tender, detection systems, fire-panels, etc..
-  If from Private Sector, should have faced External Audits, viz :- IMS, BVC.
6
Likely sources
Organization example :
- Retired Personnel from other Industries / Govt Service..
- Sector : Automobile, Manufacturing

Job Title:
Mgr - PPC buyer
Level:
EX
Function:
Manufacturing - Pantnagar
Department (Sub-function)
PPC
To ensure that parts reach the production line as per plan. Proactively inform assembly about the model mix
as per part availability.
Internal
External
Direct
Operations>Manufacturing>Assembly/Exports, Operations>Materials>Component Development, Operations>Manufacturing>Quality Assurance, Operations>MIS>Back End, Materials & Mgmt Functions
Vendors
Indirect
Operations>Materials>Raw Materials, Operations>Materials>Purchase

||

Min: 6

Automobile
Addnl Requirements
Pref exposure to automobile/auto component industries
Age
Min: 28
Max: 35
Need for travel
Medium
Technical/Functional
1.Study and enhance vendor capacity for higher / existing volume for existing / new models of Motorcycles as needed
2. Knowledge of Material accounting / PIV (Perpetual Inventory Verification) system and take care of external / internal audits
3. Ensure availability of capacity at vendor end to fulfil the required components as per plan
4. Knowledge about various manufacturing processes and systems required for automobile products, mainly Press components and fabrication components
5.Knowledge of inventory management and stores management function and systems related to SAP- MM & PP module
5.Knowledge related to excise and custom formalities
Behavioural
EX - ACT - Continuously raise the bar; Ensure results with speed; Meet customer expectations
5. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
1.Plan and procure vendor components as per monthly production plan of models of Motorcycles.
2. Ensure availability of components as per shift wise / daily production plan in terms of Quantity and Quality
3. Plan and execute changes in production plan related with cross functional departments in the plant and Vendors
4.Plan and coordinate smooth receipt and issue of vendor components to user dept in the plant as per requirement, to avoid losses.
5. Monitor Inventory and control within management norms.
6.Plan and execute outsourcing activity as per management guidelines.
7.Monitor transport cost of vendor material and target to reduce by applying various techniques e.g. Milk route run, 3PL (Third party logistics) etc.
8.Plan and resolve GST related issues in material procurement.

Job Title

2

Background
i) Edu Qualification
Desirable: B.E. Electronics /Electrical Engineering or B.E Mechanical Engineering
ii) Experience
Minimum:  8
Maximum: 13
iii) Age Limit
Minimum:  28
Maximum: 38
4
Job Role & Responsibilities
Role:  PPC Manager – Manufacturing Operations (2W EV)
Operational Responsibilities:
• Lead production planning, scheduling, and material availability to meet daily and monthly production targets for 2W EV production.
• Plan and regulate product mix using Runner–Repeater–Stranger logic and share rolling production plans with stakeholders.
• Ensure smooth material flow from suppliers to point‑of‑use while maintaining optimum inventory levels.
• Develop long‑term demand and capacity planning models aligned with supplier capability and EV ramp‑up plans.
• Manage BOM accuracy and material consumption for correct accounting, especially in engineering‑intensive EV programs.
• Identify supply chain risks (domestic/global, internal/external) and execute mitigation plans for critical EV parts.
Cross‑Functional & Change Management:
• Coordinate with Purchase, Production, Engineering, Program Management, Quality, ME, and Finance for execution of EV production plans.
• Support new model introduction, pilot builds, SOP ramp‑ups, and engineering changes for 2W EV platforms with zero non‑moving inventory.
• Track and implement ECR/ECN as per defined timelines, ensuring controlled phase‑in / phase‑out of parts.
Inventory, Cost & Commercial Control:
• Minimize excess, obsolete, and slow‑moving inventory, with focus on high‑value EV components.
• Ensure PPC financial indices are within targets through adherence to SOPs and discipline.
• Coordinate with Purchase and Finance for PO releases, logistics, and commercial resolution.
• Drive initiatives for reduction in component, logistics, packaging, and transportation cost.
Digitalization & Process Improvement:
• Utilize SAP ERP (MM/PP) for material planning and execution.
• Implement JIT, E‑Kanban, Milk‑run logistics, and inventory optimization practices suited for low‑volume / high‑variant EV production.
• Drive digitalization and automation initiatives (Industry 4.0, IoT‑based planning tools) in PPC processes.
• Benchmark material planning practices with industry best standards and implement improvements.
Stores & Vendor Management:
• Oversee store operations and ensure timely material availability at line‑side.
• Conduct PIV for plant and vendor parts and ensure timely rejection disposal.
• Drive supplier delivery performance and continuous improvement initiatives, including EV supplier readiness.
EV‑Specific PPC Responsibilities (2W EV):
• Manage material planning complexities of EV programs such as frequent engineering changes, short lifecycle parts, and localization transitions.
• Plan and control materials for EV‑specific assemblies interfacing with battery, power electronics, and thermal systems (non‑cell parts).
• Coordinate closely with new‑age and developing EV suppliers to support capacity ramp‑up and process stabilization.
• Ensure material readiness for regulatory and homologation‑driven changes impacting 2W EV models.
5
Competency Requirements
Technical/Functional
• Strong experience in material planning and procurement across multiple categories:
Castings & Forgings
Electrical & Proprietary
Press & Fabrication
Polymers & Elastomers (Plastic & Rubber parts)
• Hands‑on knowledge of SAP MM & PP modules (Mandatory).
• Exposure to JIT, TPM, and advanced material planning concepts.
• Inventory control, stores management, and cost control expertise.
• Ability to manage high‑mix, multi‑category manufacturing environments.
EV‑Related:
• Exposure to Electrical & Proprietary - 2W Electric Vehicle or EV manufacturing environments.
• Experience supporting new EV model launches, pilot builds, or ramp‑ups.
• Understanding of EV supplier ecosystem and localization challenges.
Behavioral
6
Likely sources
Automobile OEMs (ICE & EV), EV OEMs (2W/3W), Auto‑component manufacturers, Engineering & Manufacturing organizations, Tier‑1

Location: Industrial Dispensary / Occupational Health Centre (OHC)
Regulatory Framework: Factories Act, 1948
Required Qualification: MBBS + AFIH (Associate Fellow of Industrial Health)
To oversee and manage the health, safety, and medical needs of employees in the factory by providing clinical care, conducting statutory medical examinations, and ensuring full compliance with the Factories Act, 1948.
• Conduct pre‑employment and periodic medical examinations as per occupational risks and statutory requirements.
• Implement and maintain health surveillance programs for employees exposed to hazardous substances.
• Perform mandatory medical examinations for hazardous process workers before employment and every 6 months.
• Provide emergency care for injuries, incidents, or acute conditions inside the factory.
• Maintain and supervise first‑aid facilities and emergency response readiness.
• Ensure adherence to all medical and occupational health‑related provisions under the Factories Act, 1948.
• Maintain statutory forms and records (Form 7/32 etc.) as per factory rules.
• Comply with staffing requirements for Factory Medical Officers based on factory strength.
• Conduct employee health awareness programs on hygiene, ergonomics, lifestyle diseases, mental health.
• Participate in workplace risk assessments (HIRA, HAZOP).
• Maintain accurate and confidential medical records (EHR or physical).
• Prepare and submit statutory medical reports as required.
• Coordinate with external hospitals for referrals and emergency backup.
• Supervise OHC staff (nurse, dresser/compounder, ward assistant).
• Ensure sufficient stock of medicines, PPE, vaccines, and equipment.
• MBBS from an NMC-recognized institution.
• AFIH certification (Associate Fellow of Industrial Health) – mandatory for Factory Medical Officer roles.
• Permanent/valid medical registration with State Medical Council or NMC.
Minimum 3 years post‑internship experience in hospital or industrial settings.
• Strong clinical diagnosis and emergency management skills.
• Knowledge of occupational diseases, industrial hygiene, and toxicology.
• Understanding of Factory Act health & safety requirements.
• Good communication, reporting, and documentation skills.

1. Dealership management

7. Basics of finance
8. Project management
Behavioural
L3 - ACT - Continuously raise the bar; Ensure results with speed; Meet customer expectations
5. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
a)            Dealership profitability
1.            Planning and monitoring the achievement of the service targets in the area
2.            Execution of primary service network (dealer workshops & branches) and secondary service network (Certified Service Technicians and Power Technicians) expansion activities
3.            Identification of service gaps and recommend new dealer workshops/branches
4.            Promoting the sales of Bajaj Genuine Spares and Bajaj Genuine Oil
5.            Promoting referral sales by providing service support
6.            Handholding of dealers for the achievement of customer retention targets and overall dealership profitability
b)            Capability building
1.            Guiding dealers in the region with respect to monitoring & handling dealer network
2.            Identifies training requirements and organizes training for dealership manpower
3.            Gather and disseminate market intelligence by keeping track of competitor products as well as services
4.            Coordinate with dealers in manpower recruitment for workshops
5.            Execution of projects in the area as determined by the RSM/H.O. from time-to-time
6.            Initiate marketing programs/ BTL activities from time to time in the assigned region and monitor customer service to achieve service objectives and customer satisfaction
7.            Kaizens and Stock planning & correction
c)            Systems & processes
1.            Ensures the adherence to the company’s service standards by all dealer workshops & branches across the region
2.            Implementation of Total Productivity Management way of working
3.            Implementation and audit of Service Quality Systems
4.            Conduct Dealer Service Standard (DSS) audit and ensure 100% compliance of all dealers; initiate corrective actions for non-conforming dealerships
5.            Work on reduction in service turn-around time and no. of repeat jobs, minimize cost/time deviations, etc.
d)            Customer relationship management
1.            Enhancing the customer satisfaction levels in all dealer workshops
2.            Guiding dealers in speedy resolution of critical customer issues and work for prevention of such issues
3.            Coordinate with the service support team for the speedy resolution of customer complaints on the company website and consumer forums/legal cases
e)            Product support
1.            Continuously get feedback about product performance & product failures, perform root cause analysis, and update the manufacturing and R&D teams through RSM
2.            Involve in planning and executing new product launches and conduct pre-launch field trials
The above list is not exhaustive and could evolve with changing needs & priorities of the company

We are seeking an experienced Automotive R&D professional with 3-5 years of industry expertise to design EV powertrain components, casings, Motor-transmission cooling system, and EV components mechanical architecture. As an Automotive R&D Manager, you will play a pivotal role in creating innovative concepts, designing cutting-edge solutions, and enhancing the performance of our automotive EV products. Role Title: Manager – Network, Infrastructure, & Dealer Development
Function: Customer Experience & Network Development Business Unit: Probiking Location: Akurdi (includes Travel) Reporting To: LX
Role Purpose -
The role is responsible for planning, governing, and executing commercial infrastructure development across the Probiking dealer network. The incumbent will own LOI/LOA-led infrastructure rollout, ensure Corporate Identity (CI) compliance, and drive quality, cost discipline, and scalability across new setups, upgrades, and network expansion initiatives.
The role requires strong commercial judgement, infrastructure execution capability, and cross-functional coordination, with dealer development handled primarily from a governance and compliance standpoint.

1. Commercial Infrastructure Development & Rollout
2. LOI / LOA, Legal & Commercial Governance
3. Corporate Identity (CI) & Quality Assurance
4. Vendor & Partner Ecosystem Management
5. Financial & Commercial Oversight
6. Network Expansion & Format Deployment
7. Dealer Development (Secondary Focus)

• Examining the outgoing vehicle quality for functional, aesthetic, safety etc.
• Budgeting for existing and future requirements
• Ensuring the audits for JH, PM, SHE parameters
• Production planning, inventory management, managing supplier exigencies and ensuring timely dispatch of vehicles
• Knowledge of new product development, meeting requirements of assembly & testing process planning, DFM/DFA, Equipment planning, procurement & commissioning.
• Knowledge of vehicle assembly for fit & finish
• Effective use of Manpower, Machine and materials.
• Productivity improvement
• Interface with other functions R&D, Engineering, Materials and Sales.
5
Competency Requirements
Technical/ Functional:
1) Design of experiments.
2) Poka-yoke
3) Layout
4) Fixturing & modular tooling, grinding wheels
5) Knowledge of vehicle parts viz. ECU, sensors etc
6) Knowledge of Quality systems for product and customer side quality
7) Data analysis and statistical aptitude
8) Competency in production planning, SCM and inventory management
9) Knowledge of Manufacturing Engineering, vehicle assembly processes, packaging, technical drawing
10) Sound knowledge on supplier quality processes, NCDS, material science
11) Hands-on exposure of handling PM and FE
12) Knowledge of grinding, honing, heat treatment
13) Crisis handling and handling unrest - protecting plant from malicious external influences and internal unrest
Behavioural
Meet customer expectations.
Ability to raising the bar – innovative idea implementation with perfection & speed.
Team working.
Polite.
Good listening capability.
Provide leadership and direction to a team on 500+ members.
Develop and maintain good harmony and quality culture within and interface functions
Maintain cordial Industrial relations and handle grievances
Specific expertise required for functional area:
1)Design of experiments 2) TPM -Pillar activities 3) Man machine balancing
4)Customer requirement - Vehicle assembly 5) Develop & engage people.
6
Likely sources
Organization example
Auto OEM like Mahindra, Honda, Hero moto, VW, Ford, Tata Motors, Auto component manufacturers etc.

Job Description
Job Title:
AM/MGR (VEHICLE ASSEMBLY CVD)
Level:
EX- L3
Function:
Manufacturing Operations
Department (Sub-function):
ASSEMBLY – CVD (VEHICLE ASSEMBLY)
Office/Location:
Waluj – Aurangabad – Commercial Vehicle Plant
Ensure smooth operations of Commercial Vehicle Assembly Shop and meet the daily / weekly / monthly / yearly production target.
Daily ~1.5K – 2.5K vehicles are produced and supplied to domestic and export market. Rigorous monitoring of PQCDSME parameters for continuous improvement through TPM Way of working. Continuous interface with cross functional departments like Engine Assembly, paint shop, ME, QA, PPC & vendors for related challenges and get their support to achieve PQCDSME targets.
Closely interaction with cell members working on various conveyor lines in vehicle assembly to achieve daily output and resolve labour issues and support them for removing their pain areas.  Own/support activities related to New Model Introduction, Output/man/DPR/RFT/Flexibility Improvement, VAVE Proposals, New Technology Adaption, Engineering change (ECN) Implementation etc. On job training and development of newcomers.
Cell member strength of FA CVD is @ 700, divided in two shifts.    FA has 2 conveyors. Production is done of 2 shift basis and managed by 2-3 Managers.  Position requires working in Shifts.

Direct Reports
LX
@ 200-300 IN SHIFT.

Internal
External

BAL Waluj - Motorcycle Division. Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar (Aurangabad)
Ensure smooth operations of MCD Plant’s Export Assembly Division and meet the Daily/ Weekly/ Monthly/ Yearly Production and Export Dispatch target.
Daily 5K – 6K produced vehicles are supplied to export market in CKD / SKD / PLP Modes. Rigorous monitoring of PQCDSME parameters for continuous improvement through TPM Way of working. Continuous interface with cross functional departments like Engine Assembly, Paint Shop, ME, QA, PPC & Vendors for Improvements & challenges to get their support and achieve targets.
Close interaction with Service Providers & cell members working on various conveyor lines in Export Assembly to achieve daily output and resolve labour issues and support them for removing their pain areas.  Own/support activities related to New Model Introduction, Output/Man/IRU/ Loadability Improvement, Packaging Cost reduction proposals, New Technology Adaptation, Engineering change (ECN) Implementation etc. On job training and development of newcomers.
Cell member strength of MCD Export Assembly is @ 500+ working in two shifts. Export Assembly has 4 conveyors. Production is done in both shift basis.  Position requires working in Shifts.

Job Description
Job Title:
Mgr/Asst Mgr - ASM Service
Level:
L3
Function:
Service
Department  (Sub-function)
NA
Responsible for expansion of service network in the assigned area, achievement of service targets, adherence to service standards across all dealer workshops/branches and product support to manufacturing/R&D teams
Internal
External
Direct
Urbanite>Sales, Urbanite>Service Support,Product & CDMS
Dealership, Customers, Vendors
Indirect
Educational Qualifications
Essential: Engg- Electrical, Electronics & computer science || Electrical And Electronics Engineering
Preferred Institution: Any Premier Institute
Desired Qualification: ||
||
Work Exp
Min: 3
Max:10
Technical/Functional
1. Dealership management
2. Workshop management
3. Total Productivity Management
4. Product technical knowledge
5. Market knowledge
6. Legal knowledge
7. Basics of finance
8. Project management
Behavioural
L3 - ACT - Continuously raise the bar; Ensure results with speed; Meet customer expectations
5. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
a) Dealership profitability
1. Planning and monitoring the achievement of the service targets in the area
2. Execution of primary service network (dealer workshops & branches) and secondary service network (Certified Service Technicians and Power Technicians) expansion activities
3. Identification of service gaps and recommend new dealer workshops/branches
4. Promoting the sales of Bajaj Genuine Spares and Bajaj Genuine Oil
5. Promoting referral sales by providing service support
6. Handholding of dealers for achievement of customer retention targets and overall dealership profitability
b) Capability building
1. Providing guidance to dealers in the region with respect to monitoring & handling dealer network
2. Identifies training requirements and organizes training for dealership manpower
3. Gather and disseminate market intelligence by keeping track of competitor products as well as services
4. Coordinate with dealers in manpower recruitment for workshops
5. Execution of projects in the area as determined by the RSM/H.O. from time-to-time
c) Systems & processes
1. Ensures the adherence to the company’s service standards by all dealer workshops & branches across the region
a. Implementation of Total Productivity Management way of working
b. Implementation and audit of Service Quality Systems
c. Conduct Dealer Service Standard (DSS) audit and ensure 100% compliance of all dealers; initiate corrective actions for non-conforming dealerships
d. Work on reduction in service turn-around time and no. of repeat jobs, minimize cost/time deviations, etc.
2. Taking decisions on warranty claims
d) Customer relationship management
1. Enhancing the customer satisfaction levels in all dealer workshops
2. Guiding dealers in speedy resolution of critical customer issues and work for prevention of such issues
3. Coordinate with the service support team for speedy resolution of customer complaints on company website and consumer forums/legal cases
e) Product support
1. Continuously get feedback about product performance & product failures, perform root cause analysis and updates the manufacturing and R&D teams through RSM
2. Involve in planning and executing new product launches and conduct pre-launch field trials
The above list is not exhaustive and could evolve with changing needs & priorities of the company

5. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
1. Sales Vs. Targets:
a. Achieving the sales targets through channel partners/distributors in the assigned region(s).
b. Increasing the market share for the assigned region by providing Strategic Directives.
c. Involved in the preparation of Plan Actuals with Divisional Sales Manager for setting sales targets country wise by analyzing actual sales vs expected sales.
d. Updating market developments periodically to facilitate proactive steps to combat competition.
e Analyzing market sales data and customer satisfaction data.
2. Network Coverage:
a. Identifying the network gaps & identify prospective dealers/distributors based on regional priorities.
b. Managing the business by monitoring each dealership/distributorship in terms of viability and profitability.
3. Systems and Processes:
a. Capturing best practices from other regions and deploy them horizontally in concerned areas.
b. Timely Training to Distributors/Dealers and  staff via Store power or other measures on Sales process, Product & CRM related activities.
c. Reporting on country(ies)performance, Market Shares, Market information, Sales Forecast, Competitor Performance, Financier Matrix and Team Productivity.
4. Manpower:
a. Ensuring adequacy of manpower at channel partners.
5. BTL/Local Level Marketing:
a.  Initiate marketing programs/ BTL activities from time to time in the assigned country(ies) and monitor customer service to achieve sales objectives and customer satisfaction.
6. Stock and Working Capital:
a. Managing the funds flow to the dealerships/distributorship and ensure optimization of working capital.
b. Stock planning & correction
7. Finance:
a. Manage funds flow to the dealerships by way of coordinating with the Financiers
b. Ensures smooth operation by tying up with Financiers  for trade advances or Inventory Funding.

Ensure sub-assembly process establishment at supplier ends for new sub-assemblies, with systems, displays of IPO, SOP, Q alerts, Pokayoke check sheets
- *CBU / CKD / PLP process readiness*
Define packing configuration for new models introduced in export market.
Define container layout for CBU/CKD/PLP despatches.
Coordinate with suppliers for new packing material development.
- *Process Optimization*:
Analyze and refine manufacturing processes to meet optimal cost and quality benchmarks, including process engineering, tooling procurement. Lead ECRS activities.
- *Equipment Development*:
Design new machinery and upgrade existing equipment to improve production capabilities, and meet new model requirements.
- *Cost Management Initiatives*:
Conceptualize and lead projects focused on reducing operational costs.
*Additional Responsibilities:*
- *Team Management/Documentation*:
Supervise and mentor a team, providing guidance and support to ensure successful project execution. Ensure documentation readiness (TD, BOM, IPO, etc.)
- *Collaboration*:
Work with cross-functional teams to improve production processes, ensure compliance with safety and quality standards, and implement continuous improvement initiatives.
- *Project Management*:
Proven track record of managing large projects (Material handling automation, MES, PokaYoke, etc. )from inception to completion, ensuring timely and within-budget delivery.
5
Competency Requirements
Technical/ Functional:
Behavioral
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
Good knowledge of process flow, data and logical interpretation
Ability to Adapt and Align
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, and excellent communication skills.
6
Likely sources
Current or past experience in 2-wheeler OEMs

Service

Desired Qualification: ||
||

Min: 3

Technical/Functional
1. Dealership management
2. Workshop management
3. Total Productivity Management
4. Product technical knowledge
5. Market knowledge
6. Legal knowledge
7. Basics of finance
8. Project management
Behavioural
L3 - ACT - Continuously raise the bar; Ensure results with speed; Meet customer expectations
5. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
a) Dealership profitability
1. Planning and monitoring the achievement of the service targets in the area
2. Execution of primary service network (dealer workshops & branches) and secondary service network (Certified Service Technicians and Power Technicians) expansion activities
3. Identification of service gaps and recommend new dealer workshops/branches
4. Promoting the sales of Bajaj Genuine Spares and Bajaj Genuine Oil
5. Promoting referral sales by providing service support
6. Handholding of dealers for achievement of customer retention targets and overall dealership profitability
b) Capability building
1. Providing guidance to dealers in the region with respect to monitoring & handling dealer network
2. Identifies training requirements and organizes training for dealership manpower
3. Gather and disseminate market intelligence by keeping track of competitor products as well as services
4. Coordinate with dealers in manpower recruitment for workshops
5. Execution of projects in the area as determined by the RSM/H.O. from time-to-time
c) Systems & processes
1. Ensures the adherence to the company’s service standards by all dealer workshops & branches across the region
a. Implementation of Total Productivity Management way of working
b. Implementation and audit of Service Quality Systems
c. Conduct Dealer Service Standard (DSS) audit and ensure 100% compliance of all dealers; initiate corrective actions for non-conforming dealerships
d. Work on reduction in service turn-around time and no. of repeat jobs, minimize cost/time deviations, etc.
2. Taking decisions on warranty claims
d) Customer relationship management
1. Enhancing the customer satisfaction levels in all dealer workshops
2. Guiding dealers in speedy resolution of critical customer issues and work for prevention of such issues
3. Coordinate with the service support team for speedy resolution of customer complaints on company website and consumer forums/legal cases
e) Product support
1. Continuously get feedback about product performance & product failures, perform root cause analysis and updates the manufacturing and R&D teams through RSM
2. Involve in planning and executing new product launches and conduct pre-launch field trials
The above list is not exhaustive and could evolve with changing needs & priorities of the company

Job Title:
Mgr - ASM Sales (Bangalore)
Level:
L3
Function:
Sales
Department  (Sub-function)
Probiking
An Area Sales Manager manages the sales force within his/her defined regional territory. The Area Sales Manager is responsible for overseeing sales operations, meeting revenue/volume targets, Network Sufficiency, Local Marketing, Digital marketing (Dealer/ Local), Rider engagement and managing the dealer sales team in the region.
Internal
External
Direct
Probiking>Sales, Probiking>Sales Planning & Enablement, Probiking>Network Planning Development, Probiking>Service, Probiking>International & Activation, CMU>Brand Management , Finance>Legal
Customers, Dealership, Local communities, Manufacturers
Indirect
Vendors

Essential: Management || Business Administration
Preferred Institution: IIMs & Equivalent
Desired Qualification: Management||Business Administration
||
Work Exp
Min: 5
Max:8
Industry Specification
FMCG, Consumer Durables, Automobile, Consumer Goods
Addnl Requirements
Age
Min: 25
Max: 30
Need for travel
80
Technical/Functional
1. Understanding of sales processes
2. Dealership Management
3. Product Knowledge
4. Market Intelligence
5. Competition Tracking
6. Network development
7. Negotiation and Conflict Resolution
8. Sales Training
9. Local Digital Activation
10. Marketing
Behavioural
L3 - ACT - Continuously raise the bar; Ensure results with speed; Meet customer expectations
5. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
a)            Sales Vs. Targets:
1.            Achieving the sales targets through channel partners in the assigned region
2.            Increasing the market share for the assigned region by providing strategic directives
3.            Involved in the preparation of Sales plan with Circle Head for setting sales targets for Sales executives by analyzing actual sales vs expected sales
4.            Updating market developments periodically to facilitate proactive steps to combat competition
5.            Analyzing market sales data and customer satisfaction data
b)            Network Coverage:
1.            Identifying the network gaps & prospective dealers
2.            Managing the business by monitoring each dealership in terms of viability and profitability
3.            Identifying new towns for Expansion
c)            Systems and Processes:
1.            Capturing ASM best practices and deploying them horizontally in concerned areas
2.            Timely Training to Dealers and dealer staff on the Sales process, Product & CRM related activities
3.            Reporting on Dealer performance, Market Shares, Market information, Sales Forecast, Competitor Performance, RTO analysis, Application Matrix, Financier Matrix, and Team Productivity
d)            Manpower:
1.            Ensuring adequacy of manpower at channel partners
2.            Timely training of the Dealership Sales Manpower
e)            BTL/Local Level Marketing:
1.            Initiate marketing programs/ BTL activities from time to time in the assigned region
2.            Monitor customer service to achieve sales objectives and customer satisfaction
f)             Local Marketing:
1.            Undertake Rider Community activities in the region
2.            Facilitate and develop local digital marketing for a particular dealership by managing digital assets and generating leads
g)            Stock and Working Capital:
1.            Managing the fund flow to the dealerships and ensuring optimization of working capital
2.            Stock planning & correction
h)            Finance:
1.            Manage funds flow to the dealerships by way of coordinating with the Financiers
2.            Ensures smooth operation by tying up with Financiers  for trade advances or Inventory Funding
3.            Continuous interaction with various financers (Including BAFL) to ensure retail finance at the dealerships
The above list is not exhaustive and could evolve with changing needs & priorities of the company

Position: Firmware Engineer – Telematics
Experience Level: 5 – 8 years.
Location: Pune
Desired Qualification:
BE/ BTech/ ME/ M.Sc./ Mtech in Electronics (& Telecommunication)/ Instrumentation/ Controls/ Electrical Engineering/ Computer Science from a reputed institute.
Job Description:
Join our Telematics Engineering team to lead the development and optimization of the Vehicle Network Interface. You will be the bridge between the vehicle’s edge-level implementation and the cloud, managing the critical firmware that powers Analytics, Over-the-Air (OTA) Updates, and Remote Diagnostics. This is a high-impact role where you will not only maintain current systems but also architect the connectivity solutions of the future.

Job Description: Brake & Suspension Designer (Minimum 7 Years Experience)
Position Overview
We are looking to hire an experienced Brake & Suspension Designer with a minimum of 8 years of hands-on experience in designing drum brakes, hydraulic disc brakes, and suspension components, preferably within the 2-wheeler automotive industry. The ideal candidate will have strong expertise in casting, sheet metal, and plastic part design, including detailed 3D modelling and 2D drafting, and be capable of independently handling design release processes. Experience with Siemens NX and PLM systems is preferred.
The candidate should be a team player, highly collaborative, and self-motivated, with a strong commitment to deliver high-quality engineering output.
Required Qualifications & Skills
Preferred Attributes

Job Description for Regulation Management (EX)
The candidate will be responsible for ensuring compliance with Indian and international motor vehicle regulations, with a strong focus on electric vehicles and emerging mobility solutions. The role involves active participation in regulatory discussions, interpretation of standards, and supporting type approval and COP (Conformity of Production) processes for vehicles and components.
• M-Tech, BE/B-Tech (Mechanical or Electrical)
• 8-10 years
• Good knowledge about CMVR, AIS, IS, ECE, EEC and Other international regulations of motor vehicles.
• Familiar with Electric vehicles regulations.
• Good understanding of Indian and International motor vehicle Type Approval and COP processes. Candidate with vehicle testing field experience will have added advantage.
• Inclination towards study of motor vehicle regulations and their interpretation.
• Knowledge about SIAM activities, AISC/CMVR-TSC/ SCOE activities, BIS/TED activities, standards making, etc.
• Good connect with ministries and testing agencies.
• Exposure to WP.29, IMMA activities for international regulations making.
This role demands a proactive individual with strong technical knowledge, analytical thinking, and the ability to influence and engage with both industry and regulatory stakeholders.

Job Title: DevSecOps
Role Overview
We are seeking an experienced candidate in DevSecOps to architect, implement, and operationalize secure CI/CD pipelines, cloud environments, Terraform-based infrastructure, and security guardrails across mobile and cloud application teams. The ideal candidate is hands‑on with GitLab pipelines, AWS cloud environments, security tooling, IaC, and performance/scalability tuning. This role will initially be an individual contributor and later grow into a small DevOps/Platform Engineering team lead.
Key Responsibilities
1. CI/CD Pipeline Engineering (GitLab)
2. Cloud Deployment, Operations & Security (AWS)
3. Infrastructure as Code (Terraform)
4. GitOps, Deployment Automation & Tooling
5. Security, Compliance & Governance
Required Technical Skills
Soft Skills

Job Description for Regulation Management (EX)
The candidate will be responsible for ensuring compliance with Indian and international motor vehicle regulations, with a strong focus on electric vehicles and emerging mobility solutions. The role involves active participation in regulatory discussions, interpretation of standards, and supporting type approval and COP (Conformity of Production) processes for vehicles and components.
• M-Tech, BE/B-Tech (Mechanical or Electrical)
• 8-10 years
• Good knowledge about CMVR, AIS, IS, ECE, EEC and Other international regulations of motor vehicles.
• Familiar with Electric vehicles regulations.
• Good understanding of Indian and International motor vehicle Type Approval and COP processes. Candidate with vehicle testing field experience will have added advantage.
• Inclination towards study of motor vehicle regulations and their interpretation.
• Knowledge about SIAM activities, AISC/CMVR-TSC/ SCOE activities, BIS/TED activities, standards making, etc.
• Good connect with ministries and testing agencies.
• Exposure to WP.29, IMMA activities for international regulations making.
This role demands a proactive individual with strong technical knowledge, analytical thinking, and the ability to influence and engage with both industry and regulatory stakeholders.

Position:
Firmware Developer
Experience Level:
5 – 10 years.
Required Exposure:
Preferred add-ons:
Job profile:
The selected candidate shall participate in efforts to develop the firmware for ARM Cortex-M micro-controllers targeted at control units for Electric Vehicles. The role involves design and development of software based on proprietary templates that need to be targeted and tested on hardware. The selected candidate shall therefore participate in development of the various software components including low-level drivers, scheduler, boot-loader, service functions and communication protocol stacks. In doing so, he/ she shall have to consult data-sheets/ application notes as well as experts from the micro-controller vendor to bring up the drivers with requisite features and test these drivers. The software thus developed needs to be further optimized for performance in RTOS/ bare-metal operating environments. The selected candidate shall also be involved with bug analysis and facilitate quick addressing by developing software that is modular, scalable, and compatible with Bajaj Auto’s proprietary software architecture stack. He/ she needs to be familiar with relevant guidelines such as MISRA to ensure compliance, participate in design and code reviews, and collaborate with other team members to ensure that all software is adequately tested.
The task includes creating documentation for the code, including user manuals and technical documentation
Ideal candidate shall have a disciplined approach to development, detail – oriented approach to problem solving, and shall also be an effective communicator for conducting training sessions and develop lucid documentation.
Desired Qualification:
BE/ BTech/ ME/ Mtech/ MSc in Computer Science/ VLSI/ Embedded/ Electronics (& Telecommunication)/ Instrumentation & Control from reputed institute.

Position:
Embedded Software Integration engineer
Experience Level:
4 - 10 years.
Required Exposure:
Preferred add-ons:
Job profile:
The selected candidate shall work closely with the hardware development team to configure BAL proprietary architecture on a given ECU based on application need. While doing so, he/ she may need to decompose the application requirement into multiple functional blocks interfaced with each other. This would involve partitioning the functional requirement into implementation blocks, defining interfaces for each block, and analytically deriving priority of execution for each block. Subsequent to this, the function development, i.e. implementation of each block, may be handled by a separate function development team who needs to be supported by the selected candidate. Such support may include creation of low-level code packaged into S functions to improve CPU utilization or development of certain re-usable modules. Thus, he/ she may need to support incorporation of specific S-functions into the Matlab – Simulink development environment used for function development. In addition, the selected candidate shall support basic validation and configure the build tool-chain before handover to function development group. During function development, the selected candidate shall provide support for architecture and debug of vehicle level issues. He/she shall aid root cause analysis of vehicle issues. The selected candidate shall also be responsible for creating release software for ECU based on inputs from test & validation group and function development. Therefore, he/ she needs to collaborate closely with function development, and test and verification groups.
Desired Qualification:
BE/ BTech/ ME/ Mtech/ MSc in Computer Science/ Electrical/ Electronics (& Telecommunication)/ Instrumentation & Control from reputed institute.

Job Title: Electrical Battery Integration Engineer
Role Overview: Lead the electrical design and integration of high-voltage (HV) battery packs and systems for next-generation electric vehicles. Ensure electrical safety, performance, reliability, and compliance with automotive and global standards. Collaborate cross-functionally on the seamless integration of battery packs from prototype to production.
Key Responsibilities:
• Own the development, analysis, and integration of battery electrical architectures, including high-voltage (HV) and low-voltage (LV) domains, busbars, harness, connectors, and current sensing solutions.
• Develop integration strategies for Battery Management Systems (BMS) hardware: including voltage, current, temperature monitoring, cell balancing, isolation, and diagnostics.
• Ensure all electrical interfaces—HV, LV, data/signal—are robust, reliable, and manufacturable. • Validate electrical design against safety standards (AIS, ISO, IEC, UL); coordinate EMI/EMC and functional safety (ISO 26262) compliance.
• Lead prototype builds, testing, and commissioning of integrated battery systems; resolve issues in HV design, relay, fuse selection, interlocks, grounding, and insulation resistance.
• Optimize electrical layouts for cost, manufacturability, efficiency, and system-level integration.
• Oversee cable/harness design, connector selection, and electrical safety measures for crash, thermal, and environmental requirements (water/dust ingress, vibration).
• Support integration of control strategies: CAN, LIN, FlexRay communication, interface with vehicle control units (VCU), and diagnostics platforms.
• Maintain detailed documentation: electrical schematics, wiring diagrams, interface specifications, FMEA, test reports, compliance submission.
• Benchmark competitor solutions; drive innovation and improvements based on the latest technologies, regulations, and industry best practices.
• Guide next-generation development efforts and troubleshoot escalated integration issues during product engineering, validation, and launch phases.
• Mentor junior electrical engineers; provide expertise on compliance, architecture, and hands-on hardware debug.
Qualifications:
• Bachelor’s/Master’s in Electrical/Electronics Engineering or related field.
• 3+ years proven experience in automotive or EV battery system electrical design and integration.
• Proficiency in ECAD tools (Altium, Capital, EPLAN, Zuken), circuit simulation, and data analysis.
• Strong understanding of cell chemistry, HV/LV architecture, power electronics, and BMS.
• Experience in validation and integration of battery packs with real-world vehicle systems.
• Familiar with global automotive integration and safety standards (ISO, AIS, UL).
Profile Standouts: • Excellent troubleshooting and system-level integration abilities. • Passion for automotive electrification and safe, innovative battery system deployment. • Strong communication skills and cross-functional team experience

We are seeking a Senior Full Stack Developer (Individual Contributor) to join our engineering team. This is a hands-on role with strong ownership over both frontend and backend development, along with meaningful input into architecture, quality, and delivery.
You will work primarily with PHP and React, supported by Node.js services, to build scalable, secure, and high-performance applications in an Agile environment. In addition to coding, you’ll provide technical leadership through mentoring, code reviews, and collaboration across teams.
Design, develop, test, and deploy full stack applications using PHP, Node.js (Express), and React.js
Build high-quality, responsive user interfaces using React.js, TypeScript, HTML, and CSS
Develop and maintain backend services, APIs, and system integrations
Collaborate with product owners and stakeholders to translate requirements into technical solutions
Contribute to architectural decisions and ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable
Ensure code quality through best practices, reviews, and automated testing
Support CI/CD pipelines, deployments, and release processes
Troubleshoot complex issues and optimize application performance
Mentor junior developers and provide technical guidance
Participate in Agile ceremonies (Scrum/Kanban)
Work closely with QA, DevOps, and project management teams
Participate in technical interviews and onboarding
8+ years of professional full stack development experience
Strong hands-on experience with PHP
Solid experience with Node.js and Express.js
Proficiency in React.js, JavaScript, and TypeScript
Strong knowledge of HTML5, CSS3, and responsive design
Experience designing and consuming RESTful APIs
Experience with relational databases such as MySQL or PostgreSQL
Understanding of authentication, authorization, and web security best practices
Experience with Git, code reviews, and collaborative development workflows
Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and automated deployments
Strong understanding of Agile development practices
Experience with MongoDB or other NoSQL databases
Exposure to cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Basic knowledge of DevOps practices and infrastructure concepts
Experience with microservices architectures
Working knowledge of Python
Experience with enterprise-scale applications
Familiarity with tools such as JIRA or Azure DevOps
Proven ability to mentor and guide developers
Strong ownership, planning, and delivery mindset
Excellent communication and collaboration skills
Strong problem-solving and decision-making abilities
Comfortable working independently and in cross-functional teams

Travel:- This is a Sales role. Travel to the relevant countries is a must . % of travel will depend upon lot of factors such as the Covid / other travel restrictions.
Location:- Akurdi
Region :- Countries or cluster of countries in MENA
Job Description
Job Title:
Mgr - Sales
Level:
EX
Function:
2 & 3 W Sales – MENA
Department  (Sub-function)
NA
Sales Manager, International  manages the sales force within his defined country(ies). Sales Manager is responsible for overseeing sales operations, meeting revenue/volume targets and managing the sales team in (of the distributor(s)) in the team.
Internal
External
Direct
Export BU>Marketing,Business Planning & NMD
Dealership, Distributors
Indirect
Export BU>Operations, Export BU>Sales (Africa), Export BU>Marketing,Business Planning & NMD
Financial institutions
Educational Qualifications
Essential: Management || Business Management
Preferred Institution: Any Premier Insitute
Desired Qualification: Management||Business Management
Graduate||Any stream/branch
Work Exp
Min: 3
Max:10
Industry Specification
Automobile, FMCG
Addnl Requirements
Age
Min: 23
Max: 32
Need for travel
Yes
Technical/Functional
1. Distributor Management.
2. Sales Process Understanding.
3. Understanding of Sales Process
4. Creative thinking ability
5. Analytical Skills
6. Ability to present and articulate ideas clearly
Behavioural
EX - ACT - Continuously raise the bar; Ensure results with speed; Meet customer expectations
5. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
1. Sales Vs. Targets:
a. Achieving the sales targets through channel partners/distributors in the assigned region(s).
b. Increasing the market share for the assigned region by providing Strategic Directives.
c. Involved in the preparation of Plan Actuals with Divisional Sales Manager for setting sales targets country wise by analyzing actual sales vs expected sales.
d. Updating market developments periodically to facilitate proactive steps to combat competition.
e Analyzing market sales data and customer satisfaction data.
2. Network Coverage:
a. Identifying the network gaps & identify prospective dealers/distributors based on regional priorities.
b. Managing the business by monitoring each dealership/distributorship in terms of viability and profitability.
3. Systems and Processes:
a. Capturing best practices from other regions and deploy them horizontally in concerned areas.
b. Timely Training to Distributors/Dealers and  staff via Store power or other measures on Sales process, Product & CRM related activities.
c. Reporting on country(ies)performance, Market Shares, Market information, Sales Forecast, Competitor Performance, Financier Matrix and Team Productivity.
4. Manpower:
a. Ensuring adequacy of manpower at channel partners.
5. BTL/Local Level Marketing:
a.  Initiate marketing programs/ BTL activities from time to time in the assigned country(ies) and monitor customer service to achieve sales objectives and customer satisfaction.
6. Stock and Working Capital:
a. Managing the funds flow to the dealerships/distributorship and ensure optimization of working capital.
b. Stock planning & correction
7. Finance:
a. Manage funds flow to the dealerships by way of coordinating with the Financiers
b. Ensures smooth operation by tying up with Financiers  for trade advances or Inventory Funding.
The above list is not exhaustive and could evolve with changing needs & priorities of the company

Job Description
Job Title:
AM / MGR (Environment)
Level:
L3
Function:
Operations
Department (Sub-function):
FACILITIES ENGINEERING
Office/Location:
Waluj – Aurangabad
1. JOB PURPOSE
1. Responsible for ensuring waste management equipment and disposal systems are within the prescribed
parameters as consented by the government authorities including state pollution control board
2. Represent plant for all environment related obligations / issue at respective tribunals
3. Responsible of timely compliances / submission of returns related to environment
4. Responsible for creating awareness in employees regarding environment related aspects of factory and its legal
compliances
5. Initiate proposals of utilizing waste generated in the plant for recycle or reuse
6. Ensure collection of various waste samples in the plant through external agencies for analysis and
implementation of corrective actions to control the relevant parameters within the specified limit
7. Actively participate and contribute in the SHEMS and OHSAS Management system activities in the plant
8. Responsible for providing insights on environmental aspects to other BAL plants as and when needed
9. Keep updated upcoming amendments un rules and regulations related to environment aspects
10. Daily monitoring of effluent quality for deciding chemical doses for treatment
11. Procurement of ETP/STP Chemicals required for regular testing
12. Liaison with MPCB and undertake joint vigilance audit with MPCB at STP / ETP/ RO / MEE
2. REPORTING RELATIONSHIPS
Reporting to
Direct Reports
Divn Mgr (Facility Engineering)
NA (Have to manage contractors)
3. INTERACTION WITH STAKEHOLDERS
Internal
External
Direct
Production & Maintenance Dept
Mfg Engineering
PPC/Purchase / Spares Opn
SS – Security / Personnel IR / Admn / DIMH
HR / Finance
Vendors & Service providers
Government officials from DISH / Factory Inspector office / MIDC
Indirect
All departments in the plant
4. JOB REQUIREMENTS
Educational Qualifications
BE (Mechanical, Environmental Engineering, Environmental Engineering & Safety or Chemical Engineering) from Premium or renowned institute
Additional Diploma in Safety is preferred
Work Exp
Min:  5 Years
Max: 8 Years
Industry Specification
Preferably from Automobile OEM
Addnl Requirements
Knowledge about the manufacturing of Automobile products
Preferred Vernacular Language:
English/Hindi/Marathi
Age
Min:25 Yrs
Max: 30 Yrs
Need for travel
Occasional
5. KEY COMPETENCIES (Repository & Standard Libraries for Competency Framework - Drop down options access to Admin)
Technical
Functional
Behavioral
Able to interface with cross functional teams
Team working
Presentation skills
Analytical skills
6. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES (Role Based - Std Library)
1. To upkeep the waste management equipments and systems (Operation and Maintainence) within the prescribed parameters given in the consent by the government authorities
2. Timely submission of various returns related to environment in the government portals and BAL portals
3. Timely renewal of various consents related to environment
4. Initiate proposals of utilizing waste generated in the plant for recycle or reuse
5. Ensure collection of various waste samples in the plant through external agencies and do the analysis
6. Based on the analysis of the samples, ensure implementation of corrective actions to control the relevant parameters within the specified limit
7. Actively participate and contribute in the SHEMS and OHSAS Management system activities in the plant

1
Job Title
MGR - Manufacturing Engineering
2
Reporting
Functional reporting to        :  GM - Manufacturing Engineering
Desirable: Engineering Graduate –  B.Tech / M.Tech in Mechanical Engineering (preferably, but EE also acceptable) preferably from IIT/NIT
ii) Experience
Minimum:  9
Maximum: 15
iii) Age Limit
Minimum:  30
Maximum:  45
4
Job Role & Responsibilities
*Primary Responsibilities:*
Process designing, assembly readiness - robust process, high repeatability
Lead the team to ensure on-time build of pre-pilot batch, pilot batch and SOP batches.
Identify and close DFA, DFM points.
Q issues – QSR RFT defects elimination
Trouble shooting of defects using problem solving tools like 7QC tools, New models Quality (RAR-Run at rate, DRBFM, PRBFM) Why-Why analysis etc
Capacity generation as and when required in stringent time frame
Assembly related ECR monitoring and implementation.
- *Supplier Engagement*:
Ensure sub-assembly process establishment at supplier ends for new component development, new sub-assemblies, with systems, displays of IPO, SOP, Q alerts, Pokayoke check sheets
- *Process Optimization*:
Analyse and refine manufacturing processes to meet optimal cost and quality benchmarks, including process engineering, component development, tooling procurement. Lead ECRS activities.
- *Equipment Development*:
Design new machinery and upgrade existing equipment to improve production capabilities and meet new model requirements.
- *Cost Management Initiatives*:
Conceptualize and lead projects focused on reducing operational costs.
*Additional Responsibilities:*
- *Team Management/Documentation*:
Supervise and mentor a team, providing guidance and support to ensure successful project execution. Ensure documentation readiness (TD, BOM, IPO, etc.)
- *Collaboration*:
Work with cross-functional teams to improve production processes, ensure compliance with safety and quality standards, and implement continuous improvement initiatives.
- *Project Management*:
Proven track record of managing large projects (Material handling automation, MES, PokaYoke, etc.) from inception to completion, ensuring timely and within-budget delivery.
5
Competency Requirements
Behavioral
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
Ability to work in a lean structure.
Good knowledge of process flow, data and logical interpretation
Ability to Adapt and Align and lead a team
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, and excellent communication skills.
6
Likely sources
Organization example
2-wheeler OEMs and tier-1 vendors, Bosch, Delphi, Continental, Magneti Marelli, Kpit, Tata Elexi, Tata Autocomp, GE Aerospace, Collins / Raytheon aerospace, any other aerospace org viz. quest, defense and heavy machine manufacturers -  BDL, BHEL, BEML, Avtec, Bharat Forge, Lucas TVS, Dana Spicer, Mahale, Lokesh Machine Tool, Craughtsman, Linemar

Component Development – Forging & Machining
1
Job Title
Component Development Manager
2
Reporting
Functional reporting to: Divisional Head (CD F&M)
3
Background
i) Educational Qualification
Desirable: B.Tech/ M.Tech (Mechanical) - IIT / NIT
ii) Experience
Minimum:  7 years
Maximum:  12 years
iii) Age Limit
Minimum:   27 years
Maximum:  34 years
4
Job Role & Responsibilities
Role: Component Development – Gears and Shafts
Responsibilities:
Development of Gears and Shafts by hot & cold forging at Vendor end
Drawing Feasibility & DFM Closure -
- Part drawing feasibility Study and closure of DFM comments with R&D & Vendors
- Preparation of initial feasibility which includes Forging dies /Cold forging tool design, Tonnage calculations, Forging Simulation study.
Vendor Support & Sample Development / Project Execution -
- Selection and development of potential vendors (T1 & T2) - RFQ feasibility Study, preparation of comparative chart and selection of best vendor
- Selection & Procurement – MFGT (Machines, Fixtures, Gauges & Tools) Techno commercial discussion
- Provide strong technical support to vendors, SIR and process improvement
- Plan, co-ordinate and execute development plans within timelines at Vendor end by identifying & resolving the constraints, risks & bottle necks and quality issues.
- Preparation & review of process documents such as PFMEA, PFC, Process Sheets (IPO), SPC, MSA, FTG, FPIR.
Techno-Commercial Estimation (Zero-Based Costing) -
- Preparation of Techno-Commercials based on Zero-based costing, Calculation of Cycle time for CNC, VMC and other applicable processes
Capacity Enhancement & Process Optimization -
Capacity process improvement analysis and enhancement, budgeting and techno-commercial synthesis with Vendors.
5
Competency Requirements
Technical/ Functional -
In-depth understanding of forging and Gear machining processes -
Hands on experience with processes – Hot forging, Cold forging, CNC, VMC, Broaching, Hobing, Shaping, Shaving, Teeth grinding, teeth honing, bore honing and good knowhow of Heat Treatment for Gears & shafts
Aware of Automotive standards applicable for Gears and Shafts - Din / JIS - Gear microgeometry interpretation & problem solving - Conversant with latest trends in Gear manufacturing
Well versed with GD&T and tolerance stack up analysis, AutoCad, Unigraphics, Kisssoft, MS Office, ForgeNXT
Good troubleshooting and problem-solving abilities with proven ability in driving process improvement and cost savings (Alternate sourcing & VA/VE).
Technical & cost benchmarking with competitors in global industry
Behavioral -
- Excellent communication, negotiation & interpersonal skills with strong analytical, team building capabilities.
- Strong relationship building & interpersonal skills, possessing a positive & flexibility-oriented attitude.
6
Likely sources
Organization –
Preference for OEM’s Component development and R&D proto shop group ---
Hero MotoCorp, Maruti, Any 2W/4W Manufacturer ++

Position: Application Developer – Telematics Experience
Level: 5 – 12 years.
Location: Bangalore
Desired Qualification: BE/ BTech/ ME/ M.Sc./ Mtech in Electronics (& Telecommunication)/ Instrumentation and Control/ Electrical Engg. from a reputed institute. Post graduate DESD from C-DAC will be a plus.
Job Description: We are looking for a talented and experienced Telematics Software Application Developer to design, develop, and optimize telematics solutions for Android based platforms. The ideal candidate will work on creating innovative, high-performance applications to enhance functionality in modern telematics systems of connected vehicles.
Required Exposure:
• Understanding of Android Internals and build environment with exposure to Android HAL.
• Strong programming skills in Java or Kotlin, with expertise in Android application and framework development.
• Proficiency in using the Android SDK and Android Studio for building, debugging, and optimizing applications.
• Solid understanding of networking protocols (TCP/IP, MQTT) for IOT.
• Experience in developing Android middleware components, services, and custom applications.
• Familiarity with debugging tools such as Logcat, ADB, and version control systems.
• Hands-on experience with telematics features like GNSS, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and Cellular connectivity.
• Knowledge of real-time data handling and integration with backend services.
Preferred add-ons:
• Experience with telematics services, including cloud platforms, over-the-air (OTA) updates, and remote diagnostics.
• Knowledge of Android Framework internals and custom ROM development.
• Familiarity with cybersecurity practices for Android-based systems.
• Android UI/UX development and performance optimization.
Job profile: As a Telematics Software Application Developer, you will design, develop, and maintain innovative telematics applications for the Android platform. You will be responsible for creating scalable, high-performance solutions to enhance vehicle connectivity and functionality. Your role will involve implementing advanced features like real-time data processing, OTA updates, user commands handling, communication protocols, and seamless integration with hardware and cloud services. You will optimize Android-based telematics applications for performance and reliability, while collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver state-of-the-art solutions. This position offers the opportunity to work on cutting-edge telematics technologies, ensuring compliance with industry standards, and contributing to the evolution of connected vehicle systems

Role Overview:
Responsible for leading the design, engineering, and integration of battery packs into electric vehicles (EVs), ensuring optimal mechanical performance, safety, manufacturability, and compliance with standards. Collaborates across cross-functional teams (thermal, electrical, testing, production) to develop world-class battery solutions for next-gen EV platforms.
Key Responsibilities:
Own the mechanical design, packaging, and integration of battery systems, from concept to production ramp-up.
Collaborate with electrical, thermal, and embedded system teams to define and optimize battery interfaces for safety, reliability, and performance.
Lead CAD modeling, simulation, and prototype development of battery enclosures and mounting structures (using tools like SolidWorks/Siemens NX).
Develop and validate mechanical, thermal, and structural aspects of battery packs including housing, cell retention, connectors, harness, and electronic integration.
Ensure robust mechanical integration for high-voltage isolation, crashworthiness, water ingress protection, and manufacturability (DFM/DFA).
Support architecture definition through analysis of cost, mass, manufacturability, and lifetime performance.
Drive subsystem requirements, specifications, and design verification planning in collaboration with cross-functional partners.
Execute root cause/corrective action for integration, build, and launch issues.
Generate detailed drawings, GD&T, DFMEA, compliance documents and BOMs within PLM systems.
Guide and mentor junior engineers and provide technical expertise for solving complex integration challenges in R&D.
Stay updated on regulations, standards (ISO, IEC, AIS), industry trends, and new material/technology developments for battery integration.
Support and lead physical testing, validation, and qualification of battery assemblies in collaboration with test teams.
Qualifications:
Bachelor’s/Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering or relevant field.
3+ years experience in mechanical design/integration, ideally within EV battery packs or high-voltage systems.
Proficient with 3D CAD tools and simulation software.
Strong knowledge of battery technologies, materials, thermal management, and safety requirements.
Demonstrated experience in cross-functional project teams and system engineering approaches.
Familiarity with industry standards on EV battery safety, testing, and certification.
Profile Standouts:
Deep understanding of real-world integration constraints: crash, water/dust ingress, thermal runaway, manufacturability.
Strong innovation and problem-solving skills for rapid prototyping and new technology evaluation.
Experience with documentation, BOM management in PLM.

Min: 28

Need for travel
Travel will not be frwquent but may be involved based on business need
Technical/Functional
1. Conceptual & Practical understanding of Marketing/Brand Management
2. Deploying successful Brand Campaigns
3. Understanding of digital marketing
4. Understanding of Trade Marketing
5. Strong Analytical Skills.
6. Deep Understanding of sales processes.
Behavioural
EX - ACT - Continuously raise the bar; Ensure results with speed; Meet customer expectations
5. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
1. Brand Management:
a. Drives the Marketing Activation by through appropriate ATL/BTL marketing activities.
b. Manage product road map and life cycle.
c. Plan, develop & rollout BTL activation in requirement with the defined sales objectives.
d. Manages the product launches and activations by defining communication strategy, processes and collaterals.
e. Measure effectiveness of BTL Activities through PDCA check.
f.  Manages corporate marketing activities by handling the website and PR related activities.
g. Provides market intelligence to various partners i.e. the Communication agencies, BTL agencies, Media agencies & PR agencies.
h. Plan, develop & rollout BTL activation in requirement with the defined sales objectives.
2. Brand Strategy:
a. Understands the positioning & attributes of the brands to deploy an effective brand strategy to achieve market leadership.
b. Assesses the Marketing Plan of the organization to tailor an effective brand strategy.
c. Creates annual action plans using the full marketing mix to implement brand strategy.
3. Product & Brand Analysis:
a. Develops & Evolves product insights for lead markets.
b. Identifies the short and long term risks for the brand by analyzing market and consumer research & develops action plans to mitigate the identified risks.
4. Media Planning & Execution:
a. Plan innovative BTL campaigns & champion deployment across country.
b. Coordinate with the media agencies for the execution of the media plans.
The above list is not exhaustive and could evolve with changing needs & priorities of the company

Defect free fast product Introduction
